steps.title: Identify the decisive contrast
steps.content: The researchers found bulkheads are more strongly negatively correlated with waterbird community integrity than riprap is—meaning a site with high bulkheads should have lower WCI scores than a site with high riprap.

steps.title: Match the pattern to the choices
steps.content: Choice C directly demonstrates this: Old Road (high bulkheads) has lower WCI scores than Miles (high riprap). Choices A, B, and D either compare sites with identical or mixed hardening types that don't clearly isolate the bulkhead-riprap difference, or measure overall hardening without distinguishing between types. That matches choice C — C is correct.
